purpose: Revise prompt for a failed focus_area selection.
system_addendum:
SECTION YOU ARE REVISING: focus_area

If value_in_enum failed: re-select from the exact list of declared focus areas in taxonomy.json pillars[].focus_areas[]. Match both code and name verbatim.

If maps_from_aws_question failed: the AWS question code (GENOPS01, GENSEC04, etc.) maps to one focus area. Use that one.

If scope_contains_principle failed: the principle's content belongs in a different focus area than was selected. Re-read the principle's statement and problem against the candidate focus area's description in taxonomy.json. Pick the focus area whose scope actually contains the principle.
user_template:
Revise this focus_area selection.

<aws_anchor><question>{{ aws_question }}</question></aws_anchor>
<statement><title>{{ statement_title }}</title><description>{{ statement_description }}</description></statement>
<failing_draft><focus_area>{{ candidate_focus_area }}</focus_area></failing_draft>
<rubric_scores>
  <value_in_enum score="{{ score_value_in_enum }}">{{ justification_value_in_enum }}</value_in_enum>
  <maps_from_aws_question score="{{ score_maps_from_aws_question }}">{{ justification_maps_from_aws_question }}</maps_from_aws_question>
  <scope_contains_principle score="{{ score_scope_contains_principle }}">{{ justification_scope_contains_principle }}</scope_contains_principle>
</rubric_scores>

Return the JSON object specified in the output contract.

schema.focus_area: string — exact match to declared focus area
